title: Rhayader & Elan Valley (Wyside Club Site)

main_route.excerpt: went to Brook House Farm for Simon to check on hop-picking machines

main_route.normalized_name: Legges of Bromyard, Bromyard, Herefordshire, UK

On to Rhayader in the middle of Wales. We are camped at Wyside Club Site, [?1/4] mile from town, right next to river.

boundary_flags: Page B_0314_R is shared with the 2018 Farnham wedding trip (August 4th Saturday entry). Only the 'Wed Aug 16th' section belongs to this trip.

date_start set to 2017-08-16: 'Wed Aug 16th' matches Wednesday 16 August 2017, not 2018 (when Aug 16 was a Thursday). The bundle date_guess of 2018-08-16 appears to be in error. Trip_id retained as given.

summary: Simon, Eve, and the dog Tinder drove from Birmingham to Rhayader in mid-Wales in August 2017, stopping en route at Brook House Farm (to check hop-picking machines) and Legges of Bromyard (to collect forgotten food). They camped at Wyside Club Site, a quarter-mile from Rhayader town centre beside the river.
extraction_notes: 1) The diary page is boundary-mixed: the 'August 4th Saturday' section is a separate trip (Farnham wedding, already extracted as trip_2018-farnham-hattie-ramsey-s-wedding). Only the 'Wed Aug 16th' section belongs here. 2) The bundle date_guess of 2018-08-16 conflicts with day-of-week evidence: Aug 16 was a Wednesday only in 2017, not 2018. Date adjusted to 2017-08-16. Trip_id preserved as given. 3) Brook House Farm location is approximate — the family's connection to hop-picking places it in the Herefordshire/Worcestershire corridor between Birmingham and Rhayader. 4) 'Legges' is identified as Legges of Bromyard (a well-known butcher/deli in Bromyard, Herefordshire), a plausible stop on the A44/A465 route from Birmingham to Rhayader; confidence 0.75 (not stated in full). 5) No excursions are recorded (only arrival noted; trip is single-page). 6) No return date or end-of-trip narrative present.
